title: alsophila capensis subsp. polypodioides
intro: Alsophila capensis subsp. polypodioides, also known as the Cape tree fern, is a subspecies of the Alsophila capensis tree fern that belongs to the family Cyathea. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including the ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of certain diseases. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
